doc.name: Action Execution
doc.description: Execute @action definitions — API calls, CLI commands, functions, events, composites. Actions can be triggered standalone (POST /action/{id}/execute), through action-typed props within setObject (PUT /store/{class}/{id}/{prop}), or via CRUD provider action mappings for provider-backed classes.

doc.name: Admin URL Routing
doc.description: Deep links via URL params and browser history on tab switches

doc.name: API Storage Provider
doc.description: External API storage via @provider CRUD endpoint patterns

doc.name: System App Classes
doc.description: @app, @feature, @app_feature as system classes with @ prefix

doc.name: Auth Integration
doc.description: JWT-based authentication via auth-service with JWKS key rotation

doc.name: Auto-generate UUID
doc.description: CouchDB /_uuids generates ID when not provided

doc.name: Batch Operations
doc.description: Create or update multiple objects of the same class in a single request. Best-effort: saves what passes, reports errors per item.

doc.name: Cast on Read
doc.description: normalizeObjectData casts values and fills defaults on getObject

doc.name: Class Contexts
doc.description: Named view contexts on @class for field lists, sort order, and actions

doc.name: Class Management
doc.description: Create, read, update, delete ES class definitions

doc.name: Class Behavior Prompt
doc.description: @class.prompt markdown field describing class behavior for AI agents

doc.name: Property Inheritance
doc.description: Class inheritance with property merging from parent classes via extends_id

doc.name: Class Selector via options.values
doc.description: options.values as assoc map (object) acts as class selector — keys are display values, values are class_ids

doc.name: Schema Versioning
doc.description: @class.version integer for tracking breaking schema changes

doc.name: ActionExecutor — Client Dispatcher
doc.description: Client-side @action dispatcher: execute(action, params, context) with type routing. executeApi() via fetch/cURL with request_mapping, provider auth, endpoint {placeholder} substitution. executeFunction() via FunctionRegistry dispatch. executeEvent() via EventBus emit with payload mapping. executeComposite() sequential/parallel chain of sub-actions. buildUrl() {field} substitution. applyReverseMapping() response_field→es_field. updateLinks() per-storage _links tracking.

doc.name: AtomCollection — Reactive Array
doc.description: Reactive ordered collection wrapper: get(key)/getById(id)/find(filter)/forEach/map, add(obj)/remove(key)/removeById(id), setItemIndex(item, newIndex), save() batch, onAdd/onRemove hooks, length/toJSON. Advanced: Proxy index access (collection[0]), Symbol.iterator (for...of support), snapshot() safe array copy.

doc.name: AtomObj — Reactive Object Class
doc.description: Client base object: constructor(classId, data), .data store, getProps(), getPropDef(), _applyDefaults(), validate(), toJSON()/toArray()/toApiArray(), hasChanges()/getChanges(), save() children-first cascade, addChild/removeChild, getDirtyObjects(), _syncRelationIds(), _snapshot change tracking, _related/_dirtyRelated/_belongsTo. Advanced: Proxy GET/SET with coercion+dirty, update(updates), delete(), subscribe(cb), extendsFrom(ancestorId), getInheritanceChain(), getClassDefaults().

doc.name: AtomProp — Property Class
doc.description: Property definition class: getPropValue() type coercion, setPropValue() validation, all prop fields as typed properties, computed order_id. Type helpers: isRelation(), isEmbeddedObject(), isOwnershipRelation(), isReferenceRelation(), hasTargetClasses(), getTargetClasses(), getPrimaryTargetClass(), shouldDeleteOnOrphan(), normalizeClassIds(). Dynamic relations: static ID array → object resolution, query by owner_id.

doc.name: AtomStorage — Client Storage Adapter
doc.description: Client storage routing layer: setObject/getObject/delObject/fetchList per backend type. Auth management: setAuth/clearAuth/refreshAuth(async)/_syncRefreshAuth(sync XHR)/getToken/onAuthRequired. Storage types: local (localStorage), api (ElementStore REST), crud (CRUD provider), seed (read-only), composite (read[]+write+read_strategy+write_strategy). Provider: _resolveCrudProvider/_buildCrudUrl/_getAuthHeaders.

doc.name: ElementStore — Client Store
doc.description: Main client store class. Core: seed(data), setObject/getObject/getClass, find(filter) with $in, collectClassProps, resolveConstructor, applyRemote, saveDirty, fetchRemote/saveRemote, _resolveStorage(classId), setToken, .objects/.storage registries. Advanced: add/seedInstance/upsertObject/removeObject, getElementsByClass(+subclasses), getElementsByOwner(ownerId), getInstances/getClasses, fetchObjects(classId), subscribe(cb):unsubscribe, getResolvedDefaults, getInheritanceChain, classExtends(classId, baseId), _version counter, getClassSafe.

doc.name: Client Support Modules
doc.description: Supporting client modules: ClassRegistry (maps classId→constructor for custom AtomObj subclasses), FunctionRegistry (named function lookup for type=function actions), GenesisConverter (nested genesis object format → flat ES object array), ElementStoreClient (async HTTP client wrapping all ES REST endpoints — replaces sync XHR), BroadcastService (WebSocket real-time sync + reconnection).

doc.name: ProxyStorage — Remote ES Storage Adapter
doc.description: A storage backend type that routes all CRUD operations (setObject/getObject/delObject/fetchList) to a remote ElementStore HTTP API via ElementStoreClient. Allows any client (including backends) to participate in the ES storage graph without owning the data. The proxy is transparent — the class config points to a @storage of type 'proxy' (or 'api' with ES target), and all operations forward to the target ES server. Enables arch-backend to behave as a full ES client without duplicating PHP's storage logic.

doc.name: Composite Storage Provider
doc.description: Chain multiple storage providers with read fallback and write sequential/parallel

doc.name: Context Formatter
doc.description: Formatter template with field placeholders on @class_context and @prop_context

doc.name: Data Type System
doc.description: 8 canonical data types: string, boolean, integer, float, datetime, object, relation, function. All clients must coerce, validate, and round-trip each type correctly. Datetime is canonical for date-only, time-only, and full datetime — editor type controls display granularity.

doc.name: Depth Guards
doc.description: Max nesting depth (20) in PHP validation, admin editor, and EditorState

doc.name: Grid Property Editor
doc.description: Inline AG-Grid editor for typed object/relation arrays

doc.name: Inline @editor Object
doc.description: @prop.editor as inline object {id, ...config} instead of relation ID

doc.name: EditorState Machine
doc.description: Headless state machine for property editing, shared between admin and architect

doc.name: Tree Editor
doc.description: Hierarchical tree view editor for self-referencing objects

doc.name: Event System
doc.description: Subscribe to and trigger lifecycle events (before/after CRUD operations)

doc.name: @exception Class
doc.description: Exception types with HTTP codes and action chains for client response

doc.name: Export & Snapshots
doc.description: Export class data and create point-in-time snapshots for backup/restore

doc.name: filter_by — Cross-Field Object Picker Filter
doc.description: When an object or relation prop has object_class_id + options.filter_by: {field, source}, picker candidates are filtered so only items where candidate[field] includes/equals thisObject[source] are shown. Generic mechanism — applies to any prop with object_class_id. Example: @prop.editor filtered by @editor.data_types includes this.data_type.

doc.name: Cross-Class Lookup
doc.description: Find objects across multiple classes by ID or query criteria

doc.name: Genesis Seed/Reload
doc.description: Load classes and seed data from genesis JSON files in .es/ directories

doc.name: Health Check
doc.description: System health and readiness endpoints for monitoring

doc.name: Array Modes — indexed/assoc/false
doc.description: Extended is_array from boolean to support indexed arrays and associative key-value maps

doc.name: @log Class
doc.description: System log entries persisted in store, auto-broadcast via WebSocket

doc.name: @obj_ref Dynamic Typed Value
doc.description: Dynamic typed value whose type is determined by a referenced @prop. ref=self uses the parent objects own type.

doc.name: Object CRUD
doc.description: Create, read, update, delete objects within ES classes. setObject is the canonical upsert: POST /store/{class} for new, PUT /store/{class}/{id} for update.

doc.name: Polymorphic @prop
doc.description: @prop child classes (@prop_string, @prop_number, @prop_boolean, @prop_datetime, @prop_object, @prop_relation, @prop_function) extending @prop via extends_id

doc.name: Property Contexts
doc.description: Per-context overrides on @prop for visibility, editability, label, editor, and display order

doc.name: @prop_flags Inline Object
doc.description: Property behavior flags as inline @prop_flags object: required, readonly, hidden, create_only, server_only, master_only

doc.name: Provider & CRUD Mapping
doc.description: Map ES classes to external APIs via @provider and crud_provider definitions

doc.name: Query & Pagination
doc.description: Query objects with filters, sorting, and pagination

doc.name: Redis Storage Provider
doc.description: Redis-based key-value storage using raw RESP protocol

doc.name: RelationEditor
doc.description: Shared relation field logic: getOptions, resolve, filter

doc.name: Set With Relation
doc.description: setObject that also cascades relation links. Relation fields can contain string IDs (existing objects) or nested objects (auto-saved first, replaced with ID). Same endpoints as object_crud — no new endpoint needed once server handles cascade.

doc.name: Storage Backends
doc.description: Pluggable storage backends — CouchDB, MySQL, JSON file, local, composite

doc.name: Auto-strip server_only
doc.description: toApiArray automatically strips server_only fields from responses

doc.name: Unique Field Constraints
doc.description: @class.unique array of {id, fields[]} enforced on setObject

doc.name: WebSocket Activity Dialog
doc.description: Real-time WebSocket activity dialog showing changes, logs, errors

doc.name: WebSocket Broadcast
doc.description: Real-time broadcast via @changes objects through WebSocket server
